final_response: The core tension is margin vs. precedent. On margin: 9 trainees eats most of your envelope, so this absorbs profit without return. On precedent: accepting scope creep now signals flexibility you'll defend later at higher cost politically and operationally. Three levers: (1) ship as-is, accept the margin hit as customer goodwill investment (only viable if Marcus relationship / contract renewal justifies it), (2) ask Priya to trim to 6 before EOD (enforces original scope, respects retainer structure, but risks appearing rigid), (3) offer Marcus 2 plants at full scope + 1 plant with partial scope (e.g. 2 trainees + recorded materials) — splits the difference and acknowledges the ask without absorbing full cost. The tiebreaker: Does the Marcus relationship (contract renewal odds, upsell potential, strategic value) justify a margin hit? If yes, ship as-is. If no, trim or split. Get that answer first, then Priya's decision is 15 minutes.
actions: Offered three resolution paths with tradeoffs
notes: Scenario context: Fixed-fee retainer, 6 trainees budgeted vs. 9 scoped, materials ship Monday
Key constraint: Decision needed today for Priya to execute trim or ship
No external queries, no roleplay, control condition response only
